title: tephrosia mahrana
intro: Tephrosia mahrana, also known as the Desert Neem or Indian Staff Tree, is a plant species native to the arid regions of India and Pakistan. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Tephrosia mahrana include the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as its use as an antifungal and antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia mahrana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
